meta.notes: v1.3: Calendar refilled after May 9 exhaustion. 6-week arc starting 2026-05-20. Theme arc: Weeks 1-2 Launch & Awareness + Founder POV, Week 3 Agricultural Land, Week 4 Village Restoration, Week 5 Health & Wellness, Week 6 Investor Case + EOI Mechanics. Cadence: IG Mon/Wed/Fri, FB Wed/Fri, LinkedIn Tue/Thu.

weekly_cadence.notes: LinkedIn avoids Mon/Wed/Fri to dodge Flow OS collision. Crete cadence approx 8 posts/week across all platforms.

content_slots.brief: Reintroduce Crete Projects after the launch gap. One-line headline approach: three pillars under one roof in Crete. Confident reintroduction, not apologetic. Mention agricultural land, village restoration, health and wellness. Foundation phase honesty.

Long-form reintroduction post. What Crete Projects actually is — three interconnected pillars: agricultural land (regenerative agroforestry), village restoration (premium accommodation for worldschooling families), health and wellness (gym, cold plunge, sauna, physiotherapy, visiting practitioners). Foundation phase honesty about where the project is now. Invitation to register interest.

Investor-angle introduction. Position Crete Projects as an alternative asset with lifestyle alignment — distinct from typical real estate plays or fund investments. Three pillars create a tangible, diversified asset base. Speak to founders, entrepreneurs, and investors who want optionality between income, lifestyle, and long-term value.

The agricultural land pillar — what we're building on. Regenerative agroforestry, biodynamic farming, olives, herbs, citrus, honey. Short caption, visual-led. The land is the foundation everything else sits on.

The village restoration pillar — traditional Cretan stone buildings restored for worldschooling families. 2 to 8 week stays. Why we chose this length explicitly — long enough to settle, short enough to flex. Premium accommodation, not budget short-stay.
Founder voice — why Crete, why now. Honest about the Foundation phase. What pulled the founder to this part of the world. No grand narrative — just specifics.

The ICA explicitly. Medium-to-high-income location-independent families, entrepreneurs, and investors. NOT a budget destination, NOT a commune, NOT a retreat. Premium positioning. Speak to who this is for and who it isn't — clear positioning beats vague appeal.
Single-line truth about what makes Crete Projects different from a "lifestyle development". Anti-cliché — no "live your best life" angle, no manifesting language. Grounded.

The case for Crete specifically. Mediterranean climate, biodiversity, food culture, position between Europe and Africa, EU framework, generational continuity of agricultural traditions. Educational, not promotional.
Founder-built, not corporate. What that means for decision-making, project pace, and the kind of community the project will attract. Direct about the trade-offs.
A day in the life angle — what someone who'd thrive here would actually do with their time. Visual-led storytelling, light on copy.
Where the project is right now. Specific about what's built, what's planned, what hasn't started yet. Honesty as a feature, not a weakness. Foundation-phase clarity.
The agricultural land vision. What regenerative agroforestry actually means as a farming approach — layered planting, soil health, water cycle, long-term yield over short-term extraction.
The investment case for agricultural land in Crete. EU framework, scarcity of well-positioned land, generational asset characteristics. Treat as alternative asset, not as farm.
One-line truth about how the food you eat connects to your health and longevity. Punchy, anti-cliché.
Biodynamic farming explained for a general audience. What it is, what it isn't (it isn't "spiritual farming" or astrology), why we've chosen it. Practical and educational.
Agro-tourism as a revenue stream within the agricultural land pillar. Workshops, harvest weeks, structured visits. The model and why it complements rather than competes with the village stays.
Olives, herbs, citrus, honey — the actual production side. Atmospheric, no specific numbers. Show the work and the place.
Why agricultural land matters for families. Food sovereignty, what your kids eat, soil and biodiversity as inheritance. Family-angle, not survivalist.
The village as it stands now — pre-restoration. Honest, atmospheric photography. Show the starting point.

The economics of restoring traditional Cretan stone versus building new. Why the harder, slower path is the right one — heritage, materials, community fit, long-term value. Speak as a builder, not a marketer.
Single-line truth about craft, heritage, and why some things shouldn't be modernised. No clichés about "old world charm".
Worldschooling families specifically — why the project designed for 2 to 8 week stays. The thinking: long enough to settle into rhythm, short enough to keep flexibility for families on the move.

Premium accommodation as a positioning category. Pricing logic, audience fit, why we are not competing with short-term rentals or budget villas. The premium audience is small and specific — speak to it directly.
Stone, light, materials — the physical texture of the village. Visual-led, light on copy.
What worldschooling actually looks like day-to-day at Crete Projects. Structure of a day, community fit, learning environment. Practical not philosophical.
The wellness centre vision. What it will include — gym, cold plunge, sauna, physiotherapy, yoga and pilates space, breathwork sessions, visiting practitioners. Honest about phasing.

Wellness as infrastructure, not as amenity. Why physical performance, recovery, and health are foundational to the model rather than a spa add-on. Speak to operators and high-performers who already think this way.
One-line truth about location-independent founders, burnout, and what most "lifestyle" setups miss about recovery. Honest.
The visiting practitioners model. Who, how often, what kinds of practitioners will rotate through. The practical mechanics, not the marketing version.
The performance case for environments like this. Why entrepreneurs and high-performers route deliberately through places that support recovery. Not soft, not optional.
Cold plunge, sauna, breathwork — the actual protocols. Visual-led, no fluff. Show the practices, not the marketing.
Yoga, pilates, breathwork — the daily practices that anchor a stay. Atmospheric, practical.
The pattern of people who have registered interest so far. Anonymised, qualitative, no specific numbers or names. The shape of the early audience.
How EOI works mechanically. The timeline from EOI through to entity set-up and what happens between. Transparent about the Foundation phase and why we are doing EOI before forming the entity.
The asset thesis in one image plus one caption. Land plus restoration plus wellness as combined value, not three separate things. Visual-led.
The two doors into Crete Projects — the member experience door versus the investor opportunity door. Same project, two ways in, different commitment shapes.
What the entity structure will look like, in honest qualitative terms, given the project is in Foundation phase. Do not speculate on specifics that have not been decided.
Single-line CTA-led post. Register interest at creteprojects.com. No urgency tricks, just clarity about why now is the right time to be on the list.
Closing the loop. What someone gets for registering interest at this Foundation-phase stage versus waiting until the project is further along. Honest, not pressure-based.
